windows.transcript: centers from design house are an absolute game changer. I feel like I'm okay When it comes to getting the wick centered properly,
windows.embed_text_v2: placing pre-tabbed wicks into empty black gold-lined candle tins arranged in rows on marble counter, wick setup before pour, tin lids beside each container, container prep stage
windows.summary_v2: Empty black-and-gold metal candle tins are staged in rows on a marble counter while a hand places pre-tabbed wicks into them, one tin at a time, prepping containers before the wax pour.

tags_v2.action: placing wicks into empty candle tins

seconds_v2.desc: Wide angled shot across a white marble countertop covered with rows of empty black-exterior, gold-interior candle tins, most paired with their matching black lids set beside them.
Same arrangement, slight camera shift; dozens of empty black/gold tins ready for wicking and pouring.
A bare hand enters from the right side, reaching toward the back-right group of tins.
Hand briefly out of frame; tins remain staged on the marble.
Hand returns and places or adjusts a tin in the back-right cluster (smart-watch on wrist visible).
Tattooed forearm and hand hold a pre-tabbed wick by its long white wick string above a tin at far right, lowering it in.
Hand has set wicks into tins; thin white wick strings now stick up from several tins on the right side.
More wicks visible standing up from tins on the right; hand reaching down to set another wick into the next tin.

windows.embed_text: a bunch of black and gold candles on a table
windows.embed_text_v2: Pouring melted olive-green wax from stainless steel pitcher into rows of small black candle tins on marble counter; wicks centered by wooden wick bars, batch filling many tins
windows.summary_v2: Melted green wax is poured from a stainless steel pitcher into an array of small black candle tins arranged on a marble counter, each with a wick centered by a wooden wick bar.

seconds_v2.desc: Overhead-angled view of many small black candle tins arranged in rows on a marble counter, each filled with olive-green wax and a centered wick held by a wooden wick bar; a stainless steel pouring pitcher rests upright at the right edge.

The stainless steel pitcher tips forward at the right side, beginning to pour a thin stream of green melted wax into one of the black tins; surrounding tins already hold green wax with wick holders across their rims.
Wax pours from the angled pitcher into a black tin on the right; the steady green stream is visible mid-air as nearby filled tins sit curing with wooden wick bars centering their wicks.
Continued pour of green wax into a right-side black tin from the tilted steel pitcher; rows of black tins with green wax and bridged wicks fill the marble surface.
Pitcher remains tilted pouring green wax into a tin near the right edge; the poured tins glisten with fresh green wax and wooden wick-centering bars.
Pouring continues from the steel pitcher into a black tin on the right; the array of green-filled black tins with wick holders sits on the marble countertop.
Pour nearly complete as the pitcher stays angled over a right-side tin; full rows of green-filled black tins with centered wicks and wooden bars are spread across the counter.
The steel pitcher hovers over the right tins finishing the pour; the marble surface is covered with filled black candle tins of green wax, each wick held upright by a wooden wick bar.
